commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
And. Hebrew, "For he who presseth milk. "(Calmet)
Protestants, "Surely the churning of milk bringeth forth butter "(Haydock)
Strife. Moderation is necessary, (Calmet) in all actions. (Worthington)

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Surely the churning of milk brings forth butter, and the wringing of the nose brings forth blood: so the forcing of wrath brings forth strife. “Squeeze out the milk, and it will be butter. ” Squeeze out faithfully the two Testaments of Christ, and you will find the commandments to be as milk. Once you have been nourished with them, you may be transformed into perfect and faithful bread. Commentary on the Proverbs of Solomon, Fragment.
